title: Sales Representatives, Wholesale and Manufacturing, Except Technical and Scientific Products
keyDrivers: Wholesale and manufacturing sales reps rely on relationship-building and product knowledge to serve their accounts, which offers some protection. However, many of their routine tasks, order processing, inventory checks, pricing, are being automated by e-commerce platforms and AI tools. The reps who survive will be those handling complex, high-value accounts where trust and negotiation still matter, but the overall number of positions is likely to shrink.

taskData.full: Answer customers' questions about products, prices, availability, product uses, and credit terms.
Recommend products to customers, based on customers' needs and interests.
Monitor market conditions, product innovations, and competitors' products, prices, and sales.
Prepare sales contracts and order forms.
Perform administrative duties, such as preparing sales budgets and reports, keeping sales records, and filing expense account reports.
Estimate or quote prices, credit or contract terms, warranties, and delivery dates.
Plan, assemble, and stock product displays in retail stores, or make recommendations to retailers regarding product displays, promotional programs, and advertising.
Identify prospective customers by using business directories, following leads from existing clients, participating in organizations and clubs, and attending trade shows and conferences.
Check stock levels and reorder merchandise as necessary.
Contact regular and prospective customers to demonstrate products, explain product features, and solicit orders.

taskIntro: Not all tasks are affected equally. Knowing which ones AI handles well, and which still need a human, is how to focus skill-building.

risks.body: The two most important tasks in this role, answering customer questions and recommending products, already show automation rates of 37% and 33% respectively. That's significant because those tasks are the core of inside and routine sales work. AI-powered product configurators, chatbots, and CRM-driven recommendation engines are handling a growing share of what used to require a rep on the phone or in an email thread. Salesforce's 2024 State of Sales report found that 81% of sales teams are either using or experimenting with generative AI, with lead qualification and customer communications among the top use cases [Salesforce, Nov 2024]. The broader pattern: routine order-taking and informational sales calls are increasingly self-service, which compresses demand for reps whose value is primarily transactional.
risks.statLabel: of sales teams using or piloting generative AI
risks.statSourceTitle: The future of B2B sales: The big reframe

opportunities.body: The augmentation signal on answering customer questions is 42%, the highest of any task with sufficient data in this role. That means AI is frequently used as a co-pilot rather than a replacement, helping reps pull up specs, pricing history, and competitive comparisons in real time. HubSpot's 2024 State of Sales report found that sales professionals using AI tools save roughly two hours per day on manual tasks like data entry and note-taking, freeing time for relationship-building and complex deal negotiation [HubSpot, Oct 2024]. The tasks with no AI signal, negotiating contracts, managing retail merchandising relationships, and in-person client work, are structurally harder to automate because they depend on reading a room, building trust over time, and handling ambiguity. Reps who spend most of their time on consultative and relationship-driven selling are in a fundamentally different position than those doing transactional order fulfillment.
opportunities.statLabel: increase in meetings booked per rep using AI-assisted outreach tools
opportunities.statSourceTitle: The future of B2B sales: The big reframe

howToAdapt.alreadyIn: Start using AI tools for pre-call research and proposal drafting now. CRM platforms like Salesforce and HubSpot already embed generative AI for call summaries, email drafts, and deal scoring, and reps who use them report saving roughly two hours a day on administrative work [HubSpot, Oct 2024]. Over the next six months, focus on deepening consultative selling skills: complex multi-stakeholder negotiations, custom solution design, and in-person relationship management. These are the tasks where AI has no recorded activity and where the structural barriers to automation, reading body language, navigating politics, building trust, are highest.
howToAdapt.thinkingOf: The version of this role worth entering is consultative B2B sales, not transactional order-taking. Look for positions where the sales cycle is long, the deal size is large, and the product requires customization or technical explanation. When building a resume, demonstrate competency with CRM and AI-assisted sales tools like Salesforce Einstein or HubSpot's AI features. McKinsey's 2024 B2B sales research found that buyers increasingly favor reps who demonstrate deep understanding of their specific business challenges over those who lead with product features [McKinsey & Company, Feb 2024]. That's the skill that matters, and it's hard to fake on a resume without real examples of complex deals closed.

quotes.quote: Generative AI is helping sellers spend more time actually selling, top performers use AI for research, outreach, and CRM updates so they can focus on conversations that close deals.
Sales professionals who use AI to automate manual tasks like data entry and note-taking save an average of two hours and 15 minutes per day.

Seventy-eight percent of buyers say understanding their specific business situation is the top factor in choosing to work with a sales rep. Candidates entering B2B wholesale sales who can demonstrate consultative discovery skills alongside AI tool fluency are filling the gap that automated prospecting cannot.
quotes.attribution: Salesforce State of Sales Report, Salesforce

quotes.quote: The highest-performing sales teams are 1.9 times more likely to use AI, but they pair it with deeper product expertise and account strategy, not just faster outreach.

sources.name: HubSpot
sources.title: The State of AI in Sales: New Research From 1400+ Sales Professionals

sources.name: McKinsey & Company
sources.title: The future of B2B sales: The big reframe

careerCluster.title: Sales Manager
careerCluster.fit: You already know the sales cycle, customers, and products cold — this is a step up into managing the people who do that work and owning the numbers across a whole team.
steps: Consistently hit or exceed sales quota
Volunteer to mentor or lead junior reps
Complete a sales leadership training program

careerCluster.title: Sales Engineer
careerCluster.fit: You already handle customer needs, quotes, and product recommendations, but a Sales Engineer role adds a technical layer — you need to understand and explain complex systems, not just products.
steps: Gain domain expertise in software or technical field
Earn a relevant cert (e.g., AWS, Salesforce, or MEDDIC)
Shadow or partner with a Sales Engineer on deals

careerCluster.title: Real Estate Broker
careerCluster.fit: You already know how to read customers, negotiate deals, and track market conditions, but becoming a Real Estate Broker is a step up that requires a state license, managing agents, and taking legal responsibility for transactions.
steps: Earn state real estate broker license
Work 2-3 years as a licensed sales agent first
Study property valuation and contract law

careerCluster.title: Technical Product Sales Representative
careerCluster.fit: You already do the core sales job — quoting prices, reading customers, monitoring the market — but the technical role requires deep product knowledge of complex equipment or scientific goods, which is a step up in expertise.
steps: Study industry-specific technical product certifications
Shadow a technical sales rep on client calls
Learn cost-benefit analysis for industrial equipment

careerCluster.title: Real Estate Sales Agent
careerCluster.fit: You already know how to read customers, quote terms, and close deals, but real estate means working with individuals on the biggest purchase of their lives instead of businesses buying inventory, and you'll need a state license before you can earn a dollar.
steps: Complete state salesperson licensing course (75–150hrs)
Pass state real estate salesperson exam
Join a brokerage to earn while learning commission structure

careerCluster.title: Service Sales Representative
careerCluster.fit: You already do the core job — answering questions, quoting prices, reading the market — the main difference is you'd be selling services instead of physical products, which means no inventory or displays but more emphasis on ongoing client relationships and contracts.
steps: Shadow a services sales rep for one week
Practice building service agreements or SOW templates
Apply to service-sector sales roles in a familiar industry

careerCluster.title: Insurance Sales Agent
careerCluster.fit: A wholesale/manufacturing sales rep moving to insurance sales is a step down in product complexity — your customer relationship skills and sales process experience carry over, but the product narrows to insurance policies and requires a state license instead of industry expertise.
steps: Pass state insurance licensing exam (P&C or Life)
No prior insurance knowledge required
Expect commission-heavy compensation structure

careerCluster.title: Retail Salesperson
careerCluster.fit: A wholesale/manufacturing sales rep moving to retail sales is a step down in scope and pay — your customer service and product knowledge skills carry over, but the role shrinks from B2B account management to individual transaction-level selling.
steps: No credential required
Expect significant pay reduction
Apply product knowledge and consultative selling skills directly

emergingCareers.title: AI Sales Operations Analyst
emergingCareers.description: Leverages AI tools to optimize sales processes, analyze pipeline data, and automate lead scoring and forecasting for wholesale/manufacturing teams. Bridges sales strategy with AI-driven insights to improve conversion rates and deal velocity.

stat.text: Sales operations roles requiring CRM AI skills grew 41% in job postings in 2024, as companies deploy Salesforce Einstein, HubSpot AI, and Gong to automate pipeline forecasting and rep coaching.
stat.sourceTitle: Future of Work Report: AI at Work

emergingCareers.search_query: "AI Sales Operations" OR "Sales Operations AI" jobs 2025
emergingCareers.fit: Your understanding of sales pipelines and customer relationships stays valuable, but you need to shift from closing deals to building systems that help others close them faster.
steps: Complete Salesforce Einstein Analytics certification
Build a lead-scoring model in your current CRM using historical win/loss data
Learn Tableau through a 4-week bootcamp focused on sales dashboards

emergingCareers.title: AI-Powered Account Manager
emergingCareers.description: Uses AI assistants and predictive analytics to manage customer relationships, identify upsell/cross-sell opportunities, and personalize outreach at scale. Focuses on retention and expansion revenue in B2B wholesale/manufacturing accounts.

stat.text: B2B sales teams using AI-assisted tools for account research and outreach reported a 35% increase in meetings booked per rep in 2024, accelerating adoption and demand for account managers who can direct AI workflows.

emergingCareers.fit: Your relationship-building and deal-closing skills transfer directly, but you'll shift from manual account juggling to letting AI handle routine tasks while you focus on strategic decisions.
steps: Complete ChatGPT API fundamentals course on OpenAI's platform
Build a small pilot using Outreach.io to automate one customer segment's outreach
Learn Gong.io call analysis to identify patterns you'd previously spot manually

faqs.question: Is B2B manufacturing sales a strong career as AI tools change how companies buy and sell?
faqs.answer: Manufacturing Sales Representative scores 52/100 on AI exposure, in the middle of the range, with 114,800 annual openings and flat 0% projected growth. AI tools have accelerated lead generation, proposal generation, and CRM management, but complex technical sales involving custom specifications, pricing negotiations, and long enterprise buying cycles still require human judgment and relationship management. The 81% of sales teams using or piloting generative AI are using it to handle administrative volume, which frees representatives for the consultative work that closes large accounts.

faqs.question: What does a manufacturing sales representative earn, and how does product complexity affect pay?
faqs.answer: The median salary is $66,780, but technical sales representatives for industrial equipment, specialized materials, and engineered products typically earn $90,000 to $150,000 in total compensation with commission. The premium is in technical complexity and deal size; representatives who sell capital equipment or multi-year supply contracts earn significantly more than those selling commoditized components. OEM account managers and national account directors at major manufacturers earn $150,000 to $250,000 and above.

faqs.question: Which B2B sales tasks is AI handling, and where does human expertise stay essential?
faqs.answer: AI tools now generate personalized outreach sequences, prepare account research briefs, and draft proposals from CRM data without representative involvement for initial-stage activities. Where humans remain essential is the final negotiation on price and terms, building trust with procurement teams that have had bad experiences with vendor overpromising, and technical problem-solving conversations where the customer is trying to figure out if a product can actually do what they need. The 35% increase in meetings booked through AI-assisted outreach still requires a human to run the meeting.

faqs.question: What skills help manufacturing sales representatives advance to higher-earning roles?
faqs.answer: Technical product knowledge, specifically the ability to discuss engineering specifications, materials science, or process integration with credibility, is the primary differentiator that commands premium compensation in manufacturing sales. Representatives who add formal technical education, a degree in engineering, chemistry, or operations management, can move into sales engineering roles that pay substantially more. The highest-earning path is to move from field sales into national account management or regional director roles, which require demonstrated ability to grow large accounts and coach junior representatives.
